zotero.abstractNote: Landmark detection is a fundamental task in medical image analysis, serving as a prerequisite for registration, segmentation, and motion compensation. While deep learning-based approaches have achieved state-of-the-art performance, they typically require large amounts of manually annotated data, which is time-consuming and expensive to acquire. In this work, we propose a novel framework for real-time landmark detection that requires minimal human supervision. Our approach leverages a self-supervised learning strategy combined with a small amount of labeled data to achieve robust performance. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on both within-subject and cross-subject tracking tasks, showing that it can generalize well to unseen subjects and imaging conditions. Our framework achieves real-time performance, making it suitable for clinical applications where fast and accurate landmark detection is required.
